response:
for each index i in the array nums we need to calculate the sum of absolute differences between nums[i] and all other elements in the array except when i is a multiple of 3 in which case we calculate the sum of squares of the differences. since the array is sorted we can optimize the calculation by leveraging prefix sums. for the absolute difference case we can find the sum of differences to the left and to the right of nums[i] using prefix sums. for the square difference case we need to compute the sum of squares of the differences between nums[i] and every other element. this can be done by iterating through the array and calculating the square of the difference for each element. this approach ensures that we handle the problem efficiently even for large arrays.

***

the key idea is to precompute prefix sums for the array to quickly calculate the sum of absolute differences. for the square differences we can iterate through the array and calculate the sum directly. this ensures that we meet the time complexity requirements for large inputs. by carefully handling the condition for every third element we can ensure that the result array is built correctly. the use of prefix sums helps reduce the time complexity from o(n^2) to o(n) for the absolute difference part while the square difference part remains o(n^2) but is only applied to every third element. this approach ensures that the solution is efficient and meets the constraints.
